purpose: Separates oil from compressed air by filtering, with performance degrading due to clogging and carbon deposit accumulation

outputs.name: oil_separator_pressure_difference
outputs.description: Pressure drop across oil separator element

states.name: oil_separator_element_clogging
states.description: Oil separator element health degradation due to clogging (1.0 = clean, 0.0 = fully clogged)

states.name: separator_element_carbon_deposits
states.description: Accumulated carbon deposits on separator element

events.name: oil_separator_clogged
events.description: Oil separator element is severely clogged and requires replacement

parameters.name: clogging_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for oil separator element clogging degradation

parameters.name: clogging_rate_factor
parameters.description: Base rate factor for clogging degradation

parameters.name: carbon_deposit_capacity
parameters.description: Maximum carbon deposit capacity before severe impact

parameters.name: base_pressure_drop
parameters.description: Baseline pressure drop across clean separator element

parameters.name: max_pressure_drop
parameters.description: Maximum pressure drop when element is fully clogged

parameters.name: carbon_deposit_pressure_factor
parameters.description: Factor relating carbon deposits to additional pressure drop

parameters.name: clogging_failure_threshold
parameters.description: Clogging state threshold below which separator is considered failed

state_transition_logic: oil_separator_element_clogging decreases by (clogging_rate_factor / clogging_degradation_timescale) * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
separator_element_carbon_deposits increases by carbon_deposit_rate * dt, clamped to [0.0, carbon_deposit_capacity]
output_logic: oil_separator_pressure_difference = base_pressure_drop + (max_pressure_drop - base_pressure_drop) * (1.0 - oil_separator_element_clogging) + carbon_deposit_pressure_factor * separator_element_carbon_deposits
assumptions: Clogging degradation occurs at constant rate independent of operating conditions
Carbon deposit accumulation is linear with time
Pressure drop increases as separator element health degrades
Carbon deposits contribute additively to pressure drop
No self-cleaning or regeneration mechanisms exist
